name: canon-001-sorted-map-keys
description: Map keys must be sorted lexicographically in canonical CBOR. The input is given with keys out of order; the canonical encoding sorts them.

name: canon-002-shortest-form-integers
description: Integers must be encoded in their shortest form. No leading zeros, no over-large width selection. 0, 23, 24, 255, 256, 65535, 65536 cover the boundaries of CBOR's minor-info encoding.

name: canon-003-no-tags
description: Canonical Smart Byte CBOR never uses CBOR tags. Application semantics carried in cargo fields are interpreted by layer-2 protocols; the substrate's wire format treats all values as untagged primitives. The input here is a plain byte string that an implementer might be tempted to wrap in tag 21 (expected base64url encoding) — the canonical form drops the tag.

name: canon-004-deterministic-float-encoding
description: Floating-point values are encoded in the shortest representation that preserves the value exactly. The spec recommends avoiding floats in cargo where possible (use fixed-point integers), but where they appear, the canonical encoding selects f16/f32/f64 by the round-trip test.

name: canon-005-bytes-not-text-for-byte-arrays
description: Byte arrays MUST be encoded as CBOR major-type 2 (byte string), never as major-type 3 (text string). This matters for SAIDs, pubkeys, signatures, and any raw cryptographic material. The input shows three fields that look textual on the wire but are bytes by the spec: a SAID, a pubkey, a signature placeholder.
